---
title: "joint_histogram"
generated: true
description: "Differentiable joint intensity histogram (Parzen soft-binning)."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.metrics.information`](../nitrix-metrics-information.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:joint_histogram -->
```python
joint_histogram(moving: Float[Array, '...'], fixed: Float[Array, '...'], *, bins: int = 32, range_moving: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, range_fixed: Optional[tuple[float, float]] = None, mask: Optional[Float[Array, '...']] = None) -> Float[Array, 'bins bins']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:joint_histogram -->
Differentiable joint intensity histogram (Parzen soft-binning).

Each voxel contributes its bilinear soft weight to the four
surrounding `(moving, fixed)` bin pairs; the result is normalised
to a joint probability `P` summing to 1.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:joint_histogram -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `moving` | `Float` | `...` | — | Images of identical shape. |
| `fixed` | `Float` | `...` | — | Images of identical shape. |
| `bins` | `int` | — | `32` | Number of bins per axis (default 32). |
| `range_moving` | `Optional[tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Optional `(lo, hi)` intensity range per image. `None` (default) uses the per-image data min / max -- convenient, but the bounds are then piecewise-constant in the inputs; pin an explicit range for a binning that is stable across optimisation steps. |
| `range_fixed` | `Optional[tuple[float, float]]` | — | `None` | Optional `(lo, hi)` intensity range per image. `None` (default) uses the per-image data min / max -- convenient, but the bounds are then piecewise-constant in the inputs; pin an explicit range for a binning that is stable across optimisation steps. |
| `mask` | `Optional[Float[Array, '...']]` | — | `None` | Optional non-negative per-voxel weight (same shape as the images) gating the scatter: an out-of-mask voxel contributes nothing to the joint distribution, so a downstream MI / CR ignores it. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `bins` × `bins`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sections:joint_histogram -->
::: {.callout-note}
The soft weight is **linear** (order-1 B-spline / triangular
Parzen, support fixed at two bins): there is no temperature /
bandwidth knob, so it does **not** reduce to hard (nearest-bin)
binning at a fixed `bins` under any parameter limit.  It
coincides with hard binning only when every sample lands on a bin
centre; as `bins` grows both converge to the same continuous
distribution.
:::
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:joint_histogram -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `bins` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
